title: Definitions in the Code to be understood subject to exceptions
content: Throughout this Code every definition of an offence, every penal provision and every illustration of every such definition or penal provision, shall be understood subject to the exceptions contained in the chapter entitled 'General Exceptions.'

illumination_lamp.scenario: A child under seven years of age commits an act.

title: Sense of expression once explained
content: Every expression which is explained in any part of this Code is used in every part of this Code in conformity with the explanation.

title: Gender
content: The pronoun 'he' and its derivatives are used of any person, whether male or female.

title: Number
content: Unless the contrary appears from the context, words importing the singular number include the plural number.

title: Man, Woman
content: The word 'man' denotes a male human being of any age; the word 'woman' denotes a female human being of any age.

title: Person
content: The word 'person' includes any Company or Association or body of persons, whether incorporated or not.

title: Public
content: The word 'public' includes any class of the public or any community.

title: Definition of Queen
content: [Repealed by the Federal Laws (Revision and Declaration) Ordinance, 1981].

title: Servant of the State
content: The words 'servant of the State' denote any officer or servant continued, appointed or employed in Pakistan.

title: Definition of British India
content: [Repealed by the Federal Laws (Revision and Declaration) Ordinance, 1981].

title: Definition of Government of India
content: [Repealed by the Federal Laws (Revision and Declaration) Ordinance, 1981].

title: Government
content: The word 'Government' denotes the Federal Government or the Provincial Government.

title: India
content: [Omitted by the Federal Laws (Revision and Declaration) Ordinance, 1981].

title: Judge
content: The word 'Judge' denotes not only every person who is officially designated as a Judge, but also every person who is empowered by law to give a definitive judgment.

title: Court of Justice
content: The words 'Court of Justice' denote a Judge who is empowered by law to act judicially alone.

title: Public servant
content: The words 'public servant' denote a person falling under any of the descriptions following:
sub_clauses: First.—Commissioned Officer in the Military, Naval or Air Forces.
Second.—Every Judge.
Third.—Every officer of a Court of Justice.
Fourth.—Every juror or assessor.
Fifth.—Every arbitrator.
Sixth.—Every person empowered to confine.
Seventh.—Every officer duty-bound to prevent offences.
Eighth.—Every officer duty-bound to receive/expend property for Govt.
Ninth.—Every officer duty-bound to survey or assess.
Tenth.—Every officer duty-bound to maintain registers.
Eleventh.—Every person empowered to prepare electoral rolls.
Twelfth.—Every person in the service or pay of the Government.

title: Moveable property
content: Corporeal property of every description, except land and things attached to the earth.

title: Wrongful gain / Wrongful loss
content: Definitions of wrongful gain and wrongful loss:
sub_clauses: 'Wrongful gain' is gain by unlawful means of property to which the person gaining is not legally entitled.
'Wrongful loss' is loss by unlawful means of property to which the person losing it is legally entitled.

title: Dishonestly
content: Intention of causing wrongful gain or wrongful loss.

title: Fraudulently
content: A person is said to do a thing fraudulently if he does that thing with intent to defraud but not otherwise.

title: Reason to believe
content: A person is said to have 'reason to believe' a thing, if he has sufficient cause to believe that thing but not otherwise.

title: Property in possession of wife, clerk or servant
content: When property is in the possession of a person’s wife, clerk or servant, on account of that person, it is in that person’s possession.

title: Counterfeit
content: A person is said to counterfeit who causes one thing to resemble another thing, intending by means of that resemblance to practice deception.

title: Document
content: The word 'document' denotes any matter expressed or described upon any substance by means of letters, figures, or marks, or by more than one of those means.
sub_clauses: Explanation 1.—It is immaterial by what means or upon what substance the letters, figures or marks are formed.
Explanation 2.—It is immaterial whether the expression was intended to be evidence or not.

title: Valuable security
content: The words 'valuable security' denote a document which is, or purports to be, a document whereby any legal right is created, extended, transferred, restricted, extinguished or released.
sub_clauses: The document must be one where a person acknowledges that he lies under legal liability, or has not a certain legal right.

title: A will
content: The words 'a will' denote any testamentary document.

title: Words referring to acts include illegal omissions
content: In every part of this Code, except where a contrary intention appears from the context, words which refer to acts done extend also to illegal omissions.

title: Act, Omission
content: Definitions of Act and Omission:
sub_clauses: 'Act' denotes as well a series of acts as a single act.
'Omission' denotes as well a series of omissions as a single omission.

title: Acts done by several persons in furtherance of common intention
content: When a criminal act is done by several persons in furtherance of the common intention of all, each of such persons is liable for that act in the same manner as if it were done by him alone.
sub_clauses: This section establishes the principle of joint liability based on common intention.

title: Criminal act by criminal knowledge or intention
content: Whenever an act, which is criminal only by reason of its being done with a criminal knowledge or intention, is done by several persons, each of such persons who joins in the act with such knowledge or intention is liable.

title: Effect caused partly by act and partly by omission
content: Whenever the causing of an effect is an offence, it is equivalent to that effect being caused partly by an act and partly by an omission.

title: Co-operation by doing one of several acts
content: When an offence is committed by means of several acts, whoever intentionally co-operates in the commission of that offence by doing any one of those acts, either singly or jointly, commits an offence.

title: Persons concerned in criminal act may be guilty of different offences
content: Persons concerned in the commission of a criminal act may be guilty of different offences by means of that act.

title: Voluntarily
content: A person is said to cause an effect 'voluntarily' when he causes it by means whereby he intended to cause it, or by means which, at the time of employing those means, he knew or had reason to believe to be likely to cause it.

title: Offence
content: Except in the chapters and sections mentioned in clauses 2 and 3, the word 'offence' denotes a thing made punishable by this Code.

title: Special law
content: A 'special law' is a law applicable to a particular subject.

title: Local law
content: A 'local law' is a law applicable only to a particular part of Pakistan.

title: Illegal, Legally bound to do
content: Definitions of Illegal and Legally bound to do:
sub_clauses: The word 'illegal' is applicable to everything which is an offence, or which is prohibited by law, or which furnishes ground for a civil action.
A person is said to be 'legally bound to do' whatever it is illegal in him to omit.

title: Injury
content: The word 'injury' denotes any harm whatever illegally caused to any person, in body, mind, reputation or property.
sub_clauses: The injury may be physical, psychological, social, or financial.

title: Life
content: The word 'life' denotes the life of a human being, unless the contrary appears from the context.

title: Death
content: The word 'death' denotes the death of a human being unless the contrary appears from the context.

title: Animal
content: The word 'animal' denotes any living creature, other than a human being.

title: Vessel
content: The word 'vessel' denotes anything made for the conveyance by water of human beings or of property.

title: Year, Month
content: Wherever the word 'year' or 'month' is used, it is to be understood that the time mentioned is to be reckoned according to the British calendar.
sub_clauses: This applies to calculations of terms and limitations periods defined in the Code.

title: Section
content: The word 'section' denotes one of those portions of a Chapter of this Code which are distinguished by prefixed numeral figures.

title: Oath
content: The word 'oath' includes a solemn affirmation substituted by law for an oath, and any declaration required or authorized by law to be made before a public servant.

title: Good faith
content: Nothing is said to be done or believed in 'good faith' which is done or believed without due care and attention.
sub_clauses: This definition imposes an objective standard of 'due care and attention' rather than a subjective standard of simple honesty.
